meta.title: Leviathan — The Lifecycle Intelligence Engine
meta.description: 8 verbs. 7 gates. 24 KPIs. One graph. The agent runtime that knows when to think, when to act, and when to ask.

props.title: The Lifecycle Intelligence Engine
props.subtitle: Your agents don't need more prompts. They need gates, KPIs, and a graph that knows where they are.
props.meta: 8 Verbs · 7 Gates · 24 KPIs · 8 Execution Patterns · 1 Graph

props.content: Most agent frameworks give you a loop and a prompt. Leviathan gives you a **lifecycle** — math-backed gates that tell your agent when to research deeper, when to stop asking questions, when to ship, and when to call a human. Every transition is auditable. Every decision is a receipt. Prompts converge. Harnesses differentiate.

trend.value: research → interview → propose → capture → exec → close → handoff + work router
ambiguity · fidelity · frame · convergence · drift · circuit breaker · PROBE
sequence · protocol · strategy · judgment · fan-out · cascade · reducer · loop
3-model independent consensus on every design call

props.label: THE PROBLEM
props.title: Your Agent Doesn't Know Where It Is

props.content:
Every agent framework gives you a loop: prompt → run → check → repeat. But loops don't know when to stop. They don't know when they're going deeper vs spinning. They don't know if they understood the question or just pattern-matched an answer.

The result: agents that burn tokens without converging, capture ideas at L0 fidelity when the conversation was at L3, and confidently ship proposals built from lossy summaries.

**The missing piece isn't a better prompt. It's a lifecycle with math.**

props.label: THE SOLUTION
props.title: One Lifecycle. Eight Verbs. Math at Every Transition.
Shape
props.content:
/research + /interview

Gather evidence. Ask questions. Gate: ambiguity ≤ 0.2 to proceed.

props.title: Plan
props.content:
/propose + /capture

Write the definition. Resolve the decision. Gate: fidelity ≥ 0.8.

props.title: Execute
props.content:
/exec

Run the topology. Iterate. Gate: convergence ≥ 0.95.

props.title: Close
props.content:
/close

QA + accept + commit + hygiene + learn. Gate: drift < 0.3.

props.title: Handoff
props.content:
/handoff

Session continuity. A cold-start agent resumes without re-research.

props.title: Router
props.content:
/work

The shell. Evaluates all gates. Dispatches the right verb. You just type /work.

Every verb is an entry point into one FlowMind graph. Every skill says 'I'm here' and the graph routes to the next node. The session cursor IS your position in the lifecycle. You don't manage state — the graph manages state. You just say what you want to do.

props.label: THE GATES
props.title: Math at Every Transition
props.subtitle: No vibes. No 'looks good to me.' Formulas with thresholds, absorbed from 7 competitor projects.

props.title: Ambiguity ≤ 0.2
props.content: Shape → Plan. Five weighted dimensions (intent 30%, outcome 25%, scope 20%, constraints 15%, success 10%). Production-verified: OMX Round 1 = 0.2455 (fail), Round 2 = 0.159 (pass).

props.title: Fidelity ≥ 0.8
props.content: Capture → Propose. Did the capture preserve the resolution of the conversation? Lev-original — no competitor has this. Zoom level = fidelity level.

props.title: Frame Complete
props.content: Plan → Exec. Does dna.yaml have all 5 fields? Does execution.yaml exist? Binary check — mechanical, confidence = 1.0.

props.title: Convergence ≥ 0.95
props.content: Exec → Close. Ontology similarity: 0.5×name_overlap + 0.3×type_match + 0.2×exact_match. From Ouroboros.

props.title: Drift < 0.3
props.content: Close → Done. Composite: 0.5×goal + 0.3×constraint + 0.2×ontology. Warning at 0.5. From Ouroboros.

props.title: Circuit Breaker ≥ 3
props.content: Any → Halt. Three consecutive failures = stop. C1 Finitude enforcement. Non-negotiable.

props.title: PROBE on Uncertain
props.content: When a gate value passes threshold but confidence < 0.7: don't guess, investigate. Three probe types: agentic (subagent), human-in-the-loop (phone, agentping), mechanical (grep, test).

props.label: THE KPIS
props.title: Every Verb Has an Adaptive Brain
props.subtitle: 24 KPIs across 8 verbs. Each drives go-deeper vs move-on decisions inside the verb's execution.

props.content:
Gates tell you **when to transition between lanes.** KPIs tell you **when to go deeper inside a lane.**

**/research** watches `evidence_quality` and `source_diversity` — if quality < 0.6, it switches from fast scan to deep dive. If still insufficient, it calls a human.

**/interview** watches `question_yield` — if each question reduces ambiguity by less than 2%, it stops asking. Diminishing returns detected mathematically, not by vibes.

**/exec** watches `velocity` — if convergence score stops improving for 2 ticks, it switches execution strategy before the circuit breaker fires.

**Every KPI is a variable triple**: `{ value, confidence, evidence_count }`. Not just a number — a number with a confidence score that says 'how sure am I about this measurement?' When confidence drops below 0.7, the system PROBEs instead of guessing.

props.label: EXECUTION PATTERNS
props.title: 8 Composable Primitives
props.subtitle: Every verb uses one pattern. Patterns compose: a protocol contains strategies containing judgments with loops.

props.title: Sequence
props.content: Do A then B then C. No choice. /close, /handoff.

props.title: Protocol
props.content: Known states, known transitions. FSM. /work router.

props.title: Strategy
props.content: Try best first, fall back. /research, /capture.

props.title: Judgment
props.content: LLM evaluates, produces decision record. /interview.

props.title: Fan-Out
props.content: Parallel dispatch, fan-in. /tribunal.

props.title: Cascade
props.content: Sequential with state accumulation. /propose, DNA cascade.

props.title: Reducer
props.content: N inputs → 1 output. $ralplan consensus.

props.title: Loop
props.content: Bounded repetition with exit gate. /exec, eval-harness.

props.label: THE PRIMITIVE
props.title: Fuzzy → Deterministic → Fuzzy
props.subtitle: The 3-layer execution sandwich that makes agent graph traversal auditable.

props.content:
Every graph edge in Leviathan uses the same 3-layer primitive:

**Layer 1 (fuzzy):** The LLM reads context and produces variable scores with confidence. It thinks ambiguity is 0.18 with 85% confidence.

**Layer 2 (deterministic):** A formula checks the threshold. Is 0.18 ≤ 0.2? Yes. Is confidence ≥ 0.7? Yes. **PASS.** No LLM involved. Pure math. The seam.

**Layer 3 (fuzzy):** The result triggers the next action. Pass → advance to the next node. Fail → route to remediation verb. Uncertain → PROBE (ask a human, run a test, or dispatch a subagent).

The deterministic seam in the middle is the control. Layer 1 can hallucinate. Layer 3 can hallucinate. **Layer 2 is math.** That's what makes every agent decision auditable.

# Every graph edge follows this pattern
edge:
  from: research_node
  to: propose_node
  gate:
    inputs:  # Layer 1: LLM produces these
      ambiguity: { value: 0.18, confidence: 0.85, evidence_count: 5 }
    formula: "ambiguity <= 0.2 AND confidence >= 0.7"  # Layer 2: math
    on_pass: { advance_to: propose_node }  # Layer 3
    on_fail: { route_to: interview_node }
    on_uncertain: { probe: human }  # confidence < 0.7 → ask

props.label: LEV-ORIGINAL
props.title: Fidelity: The Gate No One Else Has
props.subtitle: Ambiguity measures 'do I understand?' Fidelity measures 'did I preserve the resolution?'

props.content:
We discovered fidelity by catching ourselves. A 4-hour design session produced 20 ideas. The agent captured them at L1 (structure) when the conversation was at L3 (runtime detail). The agent's own fidelity gate scored 0.59 — below the 0.8 threshold.

So it re-captured. Expanded 4 low-fidelity topics from ~5 bullets to ~30 specific requirements with relationships, sources, and dependencies. Re-scored at 0.77, then 0.87 after one more pass.

**The gate caught its own lossy compression.** No other agent framework measures information loss during capture. They all assume the summary IS the understanding. Leviathan assumes the summary is lossy until proven otherwise.

Fidelity = zoom level. L0 = 'we talked about X.' L3 = 'here are the exact formulas, paths, sequences, and dependencies.' The score tells you which level you're at. The gate prevents you from shipping proposals built from L1 summaries of L3 conversations.

One node updates. Its nearest neighbors re-evaluate. Each neighbor runs its own manifestation: the video editor re-renders, the developer re-generates code, the deployer re-deploys, the publisher re-publishes. 20 nodes propagate from a single change.

This is how an agent manages an entire portfolio of products. One spec upgrade cascades everywhere. Each node has its own lifecycle instance — fractal all the way down. The execution topology IS the graph topology.

And if the graph is too big for local compute? The mesh marketplace lets you buy cycles on demand. Your agent's graph is the work. The network is the machine.

props.title: Fidelity Gate
props.content: Measures information loss during capture. No competitor does this. Your proposals are built from full-resolution understanding, not lossy summaries.

props.title: Semantic Computing Sandwich
props.content: Fuzzy → Deterministic → Fuzzy at every edge. The LLM does the thinking. Math does the gating. The LLM does the acting. Layer 2 is the audit trail.

props.title: PROBE on Uncertain
props.content: When confidence is too low to gate, the system investigates instead of guessing. Agentic, human-in-the-loop, or mechanical — three probe types for every uncertain edge.

props.title: Constraint Engineering
props.content: DNA contracts that grow stronger with every validation tick. Gate ratchet (C4): constraints never demote. The adversarial network makes the gap compound over time.

Type `/work`. The router reads your task state, evaluates the gates, and dispatches the right verb. You don't manage the lifecycle. The lifecycle manages itself. You just say what you want.

**The meta-harness thesis:** A constraint is a FlowMind definition. A harness is the tooling to run it. Leviathan is a meta-harness — it defines and runs its own constraint harnesses. Prompts converge. Harnesses differentiate.

`npm i -g @lev-os/leviathan`
